provenance.source_corpus: Importance of species diversity in the revegetation of Alberta’s northern fescue prairies

source_spans.text: Our results demonstrate that disturbed northern fescue prairies are able to recover their structural elements, including vegetative and ground cover and plant litter, irrespective of the number of species in the seed mixes
Vegetative cover increased significantly between 1997 and 2008
plant litter accumulated with time for the remaining treatments
increasing vegetative cover was reflected in a decline in the proportion of bare soil found in all treatments
These simple communities were dominated by slender wheatgrass ( Elymus trachycaulus) and included a high cover of plains rough fescue ( Festuca hallii ), native forbs, and a low proportion of weeds.
fescue cover... increased significantly between 1997 and 2008 for all treatments
means of species richness, diversity, effective richness, and evenness were not significantly different among seeding treatments
means of species richness, diversity, effective richness, and evenness were not significantly different among seeding treatments
revegetated areas remained compositionally different from adjacent native prairies
The proportion of weeds remained highest in the natural revegetation treatment
